page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration is constrained to 4.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside weaker multi-core and memory throughput. The server achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1403.59 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1156.0, both falling into the average category. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as the stress-ng multi-core score of 3699.56 and Geekbench multi-core score of 2529.0, are positioned in the lower percentiles. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large block read speeds at 27.14 GB/sec and write speeds at 22.64 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the n2-highcpu-4 offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU cycles without high memory overhead. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-vCPU ratio, which restricts its utility for memory-intensive applications. The lack of local NVMe storage and bundled GPUs further defines this as a pure compute-bound, budget-friendly dedicated instance.

This server is suited for lightweight compute-focused tasks that do not demand substantial memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include basic web serving, single-threaded batch processing, lightweight text processing, and small-scale utility services.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 2 physical cores, it provides a dedicated CPU allocation but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1156.0, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics, including a multi-core score of 2529.0 and a Passmark memory score of 1052.97. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core, this instance is designed for compute-bound workloads with minimal memory requirements. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic batch processing, and simple text processing tasks where dedicated CPU access is required without the cost of excess memory.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. It offers average single-core performance for lightweight compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-4 features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM, suitable for lightweight compute-bound workloads.
tagline: A dedicated compute-focused instance with a minimal memory footprint, designed for lightweight single-threaded processing and basic web serving.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
4.0 GB of RAM providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Average single-core performance with weaker multi-core scaling
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight web serving and basic batch processing
